message.content: Read /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/v3-prompts/wp-036.md end-to-end, then implement everything it describes.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Reading the spec file first.

content.content: 1 # WP-036 — CHR-V3-05: Activity-Stream Entry + Drawer 2 3 **Branch:** `feat/v3-chr-v3-05-activity-drawer` 4 **Effort:** M — ~1 week 5 **State:** `now` (dep VER-V3-01 merged) 6 7 --- 8 9 ## Required reading 10 11 Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code. 12 13 - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §UX CHR-V3-05 entry (line 354) 14 - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md` 15 - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/collab/ActivityPanel.tsx` — activity panel (VER-V3-01); this WP adds the status-bar entry point + slide-in drawer 16 - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/collab/activityStore.ts` — zustand slice (VER-V3-01) 17 - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx` — already has lens dropdown (slot 2); activity drawer entry is slot 6 18 - `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx` — top-level chrome; drawer mounts here 19 20 --- 21 22 ## Verbatim CHR-V3-05 tracker entry (§UX, line 354) 23 24 **CHR-V3-05 — Activity-stream entry + drawer** 25 26 **Status.** `now`. T3 VER-V3-01 cousin. 27 **Scope.** Slot 6 entry in the status bar + drawer slide-in from right edge. 380 px wide, layered over right rail (dims 50 %); latest 10 events. Hover preview, click time-travel. 28 **Data model.** Reuses T3 activity-stream schema. 29 **Engine.** Reuses T3 snapshot replay; hover preview to off-screen frame. 30 **UI.** `--text-sm` event rows, `--text-2xs` timestamp. `--ease-paper` 200 ms slide. `Cmd+H`. 31 **Acceptance.** Click event → time-travel in 240 ms with floating `[restore]` chip. Hover does not commit. Filter chips reduce list. 32 **Effort.** M. 33 34 --- 35 36 ## Setup 37 38 ```bash 39 git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only 40 git checkout -b feat/v3-chr-v3-05-activity-drawer 41 cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai 42 uv sync && pnpm install 43 ``` 44 45 --- 46 47 ## Implementation tasks 48 49 ### 1. Status-bar slot 6 entry (`packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx`) 50 51 Find the `StatusBar.tsx` component. The status bar currently has the lens dropdown in slot 2 (CHR-V3-03). Add a slot 6 entry on the right end of the bar: 52 53 ```tsx 54 63 ``` 64 65 `unreadCount` = number of activity rows newer than the user's last-seen timestamp. 66 67 ### 2. `ActivityDrawer` component (`packages/web/src/collab/ActivityDrawer.tsx`) 68 69 A right-edge drawer that slides in at 380 px width: 70 71 ```tsx 72 type ActivityDrawerProps = { 73 isOpen: boolean; 74 onClose: () => void; 75 }; 76 ``` 77 78 Behaviour: 79 - Slides in from the right edge in 200 ms using `--ease-paper` transition. 80 - Overlays the right rail (z-index above Inspector); right rail dims to 50 % opacity while drawer is open. 81 - Shows the latest 10 activity rows from `activityStore`. 82 - Each row at `--text-sm`: avatar + verb + noun + timestamp at `--text-2xs`. 83 - **Hover:** canvas dims to 40 % + previews the change (query the snapshot diff; show at `--cat-edit` overlay). Hover does NOT commit. 84 - **Click:** time-travel — sets the canvas to the row's `parentSnapshotId` with a "viewing version N" banner + floating `[Restore]` chip. Click `[Restore]` → `POST /activity/{id}/restore`. 85 - Filter chips at top: `All / Mine / Comments / Commits`. 86 - "See all →" link opens the full `ActivityPanel` (VER-V3-01). 87 - `Esc` or clicking outside closes. 88 89 ### 3. `Cmd+H` keybinding (`packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx`) 90 91 ```ts 92 useEffect(() => { 93 const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { 94 if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'h') { 95 e.preventDefault(); 96 toggleActivityDrawer(); 97 } 98 }; 99 window.addEventListener('keydown', handler); 100 return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handler); 101 }, [toggleActivityDrawer]); 102 ``` 103 104 ### 4. Drawer state (`packages/web/src/workspace/workspaceStore.ts`) 105 106 Add `isActivityDrawerOpen: boolean` and `toggleActivityDrawer()` to the workspace store (or create a minimal `activityDrawerStore.ts` if no workspace store exists). The store also tracks `lastSeenAt: number` (ms epoch) to compute `unreadCount`. 107 108 ### 5. CSS (`packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.css` or inline tokens) 109 110 ```css 111 .status-bar__activity-drawer { 112 position: fixed; 113 right: 0; 114 top: var(--status-bar-height, 32px); 115 width: 380px; 116 height: calc(100vh - var(--status-bar-height, 32px)); 117 background: var(--color-surface-1); 118 border-left: 1px solid var(--color-border); 119 transform: translateX(100%); 120 transition: transform 200ms var(--ease-paper); 121 z-index: 50; 122 } 123 .status-bar__activity-drawer.open { 124 transform: translateX(0); 125 } 126 ``` 127 128 --- 129 130 ## No backend changes 131 132 This WP is frontend-only. All data comes from the existing `GET /models/{id}/activity` route and the existing `activityStore`. 133 134 --- 135 136 ## Tests 137 138 Write Vitest tests in `packages/web/src/collab/ActivityDrawer.test.tsx` (or follow the existing test runner pattern): 139 140 - Drawer renders as closed by default. 141 - Clicking the status-bar entry opens the drawer. 142 - `Cmd+H` keybinding opens the drawer. 143 - `Esc` closes the drawer. 144 - Unread count badge shows when rows are newer than `lastSeenAt`. 145 - Filter chip "Mine" filters to `authorId === currentUserId`. 146 147 --- 148 149 ## Implementation rules 150 151 - **Status-bar slot discipline.** Slot 6 is the rightmost item. Do not rearrange existing slots (lens dropdown stays in slot 2 per CHR-V3-03). 152 - **Hover does not commit.** Preview is read-only. Time-travel only happens on explicit click. 153 - **380 px drawer width.** Hard-coded per the spec. Do not parameterise. 154 - **`--ease-paper` animation.** 200 ms slide in/out. Use CSS transition, not JavaScript animation. 155 - **No hex literals.** CSS token variables only. 156 157 --- 158 159 ## Verify gate 160 161 ```bash 162 cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai 163 pnpm tsc --noEmit 164 pnpm format:check 165 ``` 166 167 --- 168 169 ## Commit and push 170 171 ```bash 172 git add packages/web/src/collab/ActivityDrawer.tsx \ 173 packages/web/src/collab/ActivityDrawer.test.tsx \ 174 packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx \ 175 packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx 176 # Add store file(s) 177 git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF' 178 feat(chr): CHR-V3-05 activity-stream drawer — status-bar entry + Cmd+H slide-in 179 180 380 px right-edge drawer (--ease-paper 200 ms); latest 10 activity rows; hover 181 previews diff without committing; click time-travels with [Restore] chip; filter 182 chips (All/Mine/Comments/Commits); unread badge on status-bar slot 6; Cmd+H binding. 183 184 Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 185 EOF 186 )" 187 git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-05-activity-drawer 188 ``` 189 190 --- 191 192 ## Final report template 193 194 ``` 195 WP: WP-036 / CHR-V3-05 196 Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-05-activity-drawer 197 Status: done 198 Tests: passed (ActivityDrawer.test.tsx) 199 Files changed: 200 - packages/web/src/collab/ActivityDrawer.tsx 201 - packages/web/src/collab/ActivityDrawer.test.tsx 202 - packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx (slot 6 entry) 203 - packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx (Cmd+H binding) 204 - 205 Notes: 206 207 Tracker: §UX CHR-V3-05 → change status to `done` 208 ``` 209

tsc: The TypeScript Compiler - Version 5.9.3

COMMON COMMANDS

  tsc
  Compiles the current project (tsconfig.json in the working directory.)

  tsc app.ts util.ts
  Ignoring tsconfig.json, compiles the specified files with default compiler options.

  tsc -b
  Build a composite project in the working directory.

  tsc --init
  Creates a tsconfig.json with the recommended settings in the working directory.

  tsc -p ./path/to/tsconfig.json
  Compiles the TypeScript project located at the specified path.

  tsc --help --all
  An expanded version of this information, showing all possible compiler options

  tsc --noEmit
  tsc --target esnext
  Compiles the current project, with additional settings.

COMMAND LINE FLAGS

--help, -h
Print this message.

--watch, -w
Watch input files.

--all
Show all compiler options.

--version, -v
Print the compiler's version.

--init
Initializes a TypeScript project and creates a tsconfig.json file.

--project, -p
Compile the project given the path to its configuration file, or to a folder with a 'tsconfig.json'.

--showConfig
Print the final configuration instead of building.

--build, -b
Build one or more projects and their dependencies, if out of date

COMMON COMPILER OPTIONS

--pretty
Enable color and formatting in TypeScript's output to make compiler errors easier to read.
type: boolean
default: true
